commentPreTextItems.html: Khaleel nearly begs for this to be given lbw, but the umpire is unmoved. Gaikwad has a word with his bowler... and reviews reluctantly. Sooryavanshi went driving at this short-of-a-length ball seaming into middle, but was beaten on the inside edge. There's no bat involved there, as he's struck on his back pad.

commentTextItems.html: clipped off his pads to beat short fine leg to his right. That is 18 runs off the first over. Jaiswal lets this short-of-a-length ball come on to him on leg, and plays late enough to nudge it past the fielder

commentTextItems.html: looks to pull again, but mistimes it towards point
six over wide long-on!! Sooryavanshi connects well this time. Short of a length, arriving around off, and bashed hard by pulling cleanly for six

commentPreTextItems.html: Sooryavanshi on strike, with all the attention on him.

commentTextItems.html: dropped by Kartik!! That was a difficult chance, to be fair. And to make it worse, the ball ends up dribbling to the deep-midwicket boundary as Kartik didn't give the chase. Shortish ball outside off, and Sooryavanshi pulled off the higher side of the bat. Kartik turned around at midwicket, and started running forward, with the ball dropping from over his head
short in length this time, and shaping into Jaiswal's hips. He clips it with his bottom hand off the bat to deep-backward square leg

commentPreTextItems.html: Henry has the new ball for CSK. A slip for Jaiswal. Will he fire from ball one?

9.33pm Welcome to the chase. It's not that 128 or a lower score hasn't ever been successfully defended before in T20s. But when you have a pair as explosive as Jaiswal and Sooryavanshi to counter first up, you know your task's difficulty multiples.

<iframe style="width: 100%; aspect-ratio: 16/9;" src="https://youtube.com/embed/k4mgyctV7Oc?autoplay=1&mute=1" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" referrerpolicy="strict-origin-when-cross-origin" allowfullscreen></iframe>

commentTextItems.html: misfield at point by Sarfaraz, and that allows Jaiswal two. Short of a length around fifth stump, and Jaiswal shifted his weight back to slash at it and luckily get two

dismissalText.commentary: Jamie Overton run out (Hetmyer/†Jurel) 43 (36b 2x4 2x6 61m) SR: 119.44

commentTextItems.html: Has a hoick, doesn't time it. Rolls to deep midwicket, Hetmyer throws it casually to the keeper. Overton ambled the first, as he finished the run he realised he can nick a second. But the throw, on the bounce, came to the keeper at a good height. Off went the bails and he ran through towards the pavillion

commentPostTextItems.html: Prasanna Venkat: "As a CSK fan, I'm waiting to see how the pitch changes in the second innings, with dew maybe providing an added benefit."
Girish: "Oh boy, RR openers Sooryavanshi and Jaiswal are going to need all of 10 overs to overcome this total."

Ravindra Jadeja Pink looks good on me. I know Dube for a long period, was bowling to him the nets, knew what he'll do. I bowled outside off, knew he'll play big shots. Wicket was sticky, odd ball was turning. Job was to put the ball in the right areas, wicket was doing its thing. You can't take anything for granted in cricket. Have to play good shots regardless of the target.

9:21pm A night to remember for RR's bowlers. It was a dream powerplay, a dream first 15 overs in fact. Burger took out the new recruit, Samson with a beauty and also got Mhatre. Archer got Gaikwad who kinda gave his wicket away by making too much room. Sarfaraz, CSK's impact sub, came out 19 balls into the innings and started well. But Jadeja was too good on a surface that offered some turn. RR may feel a touch disappointed to have let Overton take CSK to 127 from 82 for 8 but the story is that it was a great bowling performance.

commentTextItems.html: Kamboj manages to turn strike. Length ball on leg, flicked to midwicket and the 'CSK' chants return
Shows the stumps, drives the yorker on leg to long-off. Can't nick a two
Met with! No venom on the bouncer, landed very short but didn't rise much. Overton made room and pulled it, almost got a whiplash as the ball sailed over wide long-on
Bouncer seems to have hit him on the grille as Overton swivelled for the pull. It was on the peak of the helmet replay shows. Goes to short fine leg. Concussion test will take place

commentPostTextItems.html: Ashiq Jarif: "I am eagarly waiting to see Suriyavanshi  batting !"

commentTextItems.html: Edge goes through first slip but there's essentially a deep first slip too. Stopped there. Length ball just outside off, Kamboj tried to punch it through cover
Wide on a length, caressed with a flat-bat to long-off
Looks to slice it across the line, the length ball beats him and hits the thigh. Deflects to the off side
Free hit is a slower one that's helped away with one hand. Bottom hand comes off as Kamboj went for the pull, was too early into the shot but got enough for it to go over backward square leg
Pacy and at the stumps from around the wicket, defended to the bowler. 141ks. Uh-oh, Burger has overstepped

commentPostTextItems.html: Suren: "After two heavy scoring games, it's good to see a bowling dominated game. I'm sure we will have less of these unfortunately. "

commentTextItems.html: Length over off, clears the front leg and slaps it to cover
Length on leg, flicked to deep square leg

commentPostTextItems.html: JP: "should really be leaving Overton on strike. and maybe not even run the singles early in the over.. needs a few boundaries to at least give them a bit of a chance to win "

dismissalText.commentary: Sanju Samson b Burger 6 (7b 1x4 0x6 11m) SR: 85.71

dismissalComment.html: bowled him!! The off stump is flattened. Burger fires one at 140kph, and Samson is beaten after looking to heave across the line. On a length on middle, and the ball seams away after pitching. Samson went swinging without any foot movement, and missed. Archer kept the ball fullish and straight, and Gaikwad, looking to open the face of the bat and perhaps slice it, missed. The stumps lit up. There was the swag on Archer's face after launching that ball at 143kph

dismissalText.commentary: Ayush Mhatre c †Jurel b Burger 0 (1b 0x4 0x6 7m) SR: 0

dismissalComment.html: loud appeal for caught behind - and with extreme confidence from Burger and Jurel - but it's turned down. RR review, though. It was a bouncer from Burger that left Mhatre nearly cramped. The ball landed on leg, and took off to pass close to Mhatre's head. He looked to pull that late, but was beaten by the pace and the bounce. Ohh, there's a spike on UltraEdge as the ball zips past the glove. It brushes his bottom thumb, and Mhatre has to go. A first-ball duck for Mhatre, and CSK have lost three wickets already. In fact, Burger is on a hat-trick

dismissalText.commentary: Sarfaraz Khan lbw b Jadeja 17 (12b 2x4 1x6 21m) SR: 141.66

dismissalComment.html: Jurel and Jadeja are delighted while appealing, and Sarfaraz is given lbw. But he reviews. Sarfaraz went sweeping, but missed, as the ball went beneath his bat. The first replay hints that it could be too high as he's hit on his back, but we'll see. No bat involved is the confirmation from the third umpire. Ohh, in fact, the ball pitches on middle, and straightens enough for Ball Tracking to project it to hit the top of middle. Jadeja strikes in his first over

dismissalText.commentary: Shivam Dube c Ravi Bishnoi b Jadeja 6 (4b 0x4 1x6 2m) SR: 150

dismissalComment.html: number six!! Jadeja's experience trumps Dube's aggression. The RR spinner goes full and wider of off, tempting Dube to launch him down the ground again. Dube takes the bait, but ends up finding long-off. It was a bit of a cat and mouse between the two right from the first ball, and Jadeja won't mind taking the wicket after being hit for six first ball

dismissalText.commentary: Kartik Sharma lbw b Brijesh Sharma 18 (15b 0x4 1x6 24m) SR: 120

dismissalComment.html: That should be out, finger goes up. Kartik chats... and takes the review. 134ks full ball on middle and leg. Kartik's forward stride was small and he tried to drive it through mid-on. Bat well in front of body, beat the inside-edge and hit the outer-half of the front pad. On the shin. Ball-tracking shows it's hitting leg stump and Brijesh has his first IPL wicket.
